sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Save current state before clicking middle red cross
current_blocks = {}
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] == 4:
        b = n['boundary']
        top = min(p[0] for p in b)
        left = min(p[1] for p in b)
        current_blocks[(top, left)] = n['color']

# Click middle red cross at ~(26, 14)
predict(expect_change=True, note='Clicking middle red cross')
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 26, 'col': 14}])

# Compare
new_blocks = {}
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] == 4:
        b = n['boundary']
        top = min(p[0] for p in b)
        left = min(p[1] for p in b)
        new_blocks[(top, left)] = n['color']

changed = []
for pos in sorted(set(list(current_blocks.keys()) + list(new_blocks.keys()))):
    old = current_blocks.get(pos, '?')
    new = new_blocks.get(pos, '?')
    if old != new:
        changed.append((pos, old, new))

print(f"Changed blocks ({len(changed)}):")
for pos, old, new in changed:
    print(f"  {pos}: {old} -> {new}")

# Group by column
from collections import defaultdict
by_col = defaultdict(list)
for pos, old, new in changed:
    by_col[pos[1]].append((pos[0], old, new))

for col in sorted(by_col.keys()):
    print(f"\nColumn {col}:")
    for row, old, new in by_col[col]:
        print(f"  row {row}: {old} -> {new}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
